contributorEmails.subject: Join the Next.js Community
contributorEmails.body: Hi John, I saw your work on React and was impressed by your understanding of frontend development. Next.js is a popular React framework for building server-rendered, statically generated, and performance-optimized applications. With your experience in JavaScript and CSS, I think you'd be a great fit to contribute to our project, especially in areas like universal rendering and static site generation. Your input on our components and browser-related features would be invaluable. If you're interested, I'd love to discuss potential contribution opportunities with you - no pressure, just a conversation.

contributorEmails.subject: Contribute to Next.js
contributorEmails.body: Hi Jane, your background in web development and experience with TypeScript caught my attention. Next.js enables developers to build fast, scalable, and maintainable applications using React. Given your skills in TypeScript and JavaScript, I believe you could make significant contributions to our compiler and hybrid rendering features. We'd appreciate your help in shaping the future of server-side rendering and static site generation. If you're curious about getting involved, let's schedule a call to explore potential contribution areas.

contributorEmails.subject: Next.js Contributor Opportunity
contributorEmails.body: Hi Emily, I noticed your work on Node.js and React, and I'm impressed by your understanding of backend and frontend development. Next.js is a powerful framework for building high-performance applications. With your experience in Node.js, Python, and JavaScript, you'd be an excellent fit to contribute to our backend and server-rendering features. Your input on our API routes and internationalization features would be highly valuable. If you're interested in learning more, I'd be happy to set up a call to discuss potential contribution opportunities.

contributorEmails.subject: Join the Next.js Community
contributorEmails.body: Hi David, your experience with Rust and WebAssembly is quite unique, and I think you could bring a lot of value to our project. Next.js is a versatile framework for building fast, scalable applications using React. Given your background in systems programming and web development, I believe you could make significant contributions to our performance optimization and compiler features. We'd appreciate your help in exploring new areas like WebAssembly and Rust integration. If you're interested, let's discuss potential contribution opportunities in a call.

contributorEmails.subject: Contribute to Next.js
contributorEmails.body: Hi Sarah, I saw your work on MDX and CSS, and I'm impressed by your understanding of markup languages and styling. Next.js enables developers to build fast, scalable applications using React. With your experience in MDX, CSS, and JavaScript, I think you'd be a great fit to contribute to our documentation and styling features. Your input on our blog and components would be highly valuable. If you're interested in getting involved, I'd be happy to set up a call to explore potential contribution areas.

data.simpleDiagram:
graph TD
    classDef frontend fill:#4F46E5,stroke:#3730A3,color:#fff
    classDef backend fill:#059669,stroke:#047857,color:#fff
    classDef database fill:#D97706,stroke:#B45309,color:#fff
    classDef cache fill:#DC2626,stroke:#B91C1C,color:#fff
    classDef devops fill:#7C3AED,stroke:#6D28D9,color:#fff
    classDef user fill:#1F2937,stroke:#111827,color:#fff

    User(["👤 User"]):::user
    User -->|"HTTPS"| FE
    FE["🖥️ React/Next.js"]:::frontend
    FE -->|"REST API"| BE
    BE["⚙️ Python"]:::backend
data.advancedDiagram:
flowchart TD
  subgraph CLIENT["🖥️ Client Layer"]
    NEXT["Next.js Web App"] classDef client fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#ff2d78,color:#fff
    REACT["React Client-Side Components"] class client
    MDX["MDX Blog Posts"] class client
    CSS["CSS Stylesheets"] class client
    SCSS["SCSS Stylesheets"] class client
    SASS["SASS Stylesheets"] class client
    PUG["Pug Templates"] class client
  end
  subgraph GATEWAY["🔀 API Gateway / BFF"]
    VERCEL["Vercel Edge Functions"] classDef gateway fill:#16213e,stroke:#ff9500,color:#fff
    NGINX["Nginx Load Balancer"] class gateway
    AUTH["Auth Middleware (JWT)"] class gateway
  end
  subgraph SERVICES["⚙️ Application Services"]
    PYTHON["Python Backend API"] classDef service fill:#0f3460,stroke:#00d4ff,color:#fff
    NODE["Node.js Serverless Functions"] class service
    RUST["Rust Compiler Service"] class service
    WEBASSEMBLY["WebAssembly Runtime"] class service
  end
  subgraph DATA["🗄️ Data Layer"]
    DATABASE["PostgreSQL Database"] classDef data fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#00ff88,color:#fff
    MONGODB["MongoDB NoSQL Database"] class data
    GRAPHQL["GraphQL API"] class data
  end
  subgraph INFRA["☁️ Infrastructure / DevOps"]
    GITHUB["GitHub Repository"] classDef infra fill:#16213e,stroke:#a855f7,color:#fff
    CIRCLECI["CircleCI Continuous Integration"] class infra
    VERCEL_DEPLOY["Vercel Deployment"] class infra
    SHELL["Shell Scripting"] class infra
    BATCHFILE["Batch File Scripting"] class infra
  end
  NEXT -->|"HTTP/REST"|> VERCEL
  REACT -->|"HTTP/REST"|> VERCEL
  MDX -->|"HTTP/REST"|> VERCEL
  CSS -->|"CSS Styles"|> NEXT
  SCSS -->|"SCSS Styles"|> NEXT
  SASS -->|"SASS Styles"|> NEXT
  PUG -->|"Template Engine"|> NEXT
  VERCEL -->|"API Gateway"|> PYTHON
  VERCEL -->|"API Gateway"|> NODE
  VERCEL -->|"API Gateway"|> RUST
  VERCEL -->|"API Gateway"|> WEBASSEMBLY
  NGINX -->|"Load Balancing"|> VERCEL
  AUTH -->|"Authentication"|> VERCEL
  PYTHON -->|"Database Query"|> DATABASE
  PYTHON -->|"Database Query"|> MONGODB
  PYTHON -->|"GraphQL Query"|> GRAPHQL
  NODE -->|"Database Query"|> DATABASE
  NODE -->|"Database Query"|> MONGODB
  NODE -->|"GraphQL Query"|> GRAPHQL
  RUST -->|"Compiler Service"|> PYTHON
  WEBASSEMBLY -->|"Runtime Service"|> PYTHON
  DATABASE -->|"Data Storage"|> GITHUB
  MONGODB -->|"Data Storage"|> GITHUB
  GRAPHQL -->|"Data Storage"|> GITHUB
  GITHUB -->|"Version Control"|> CIRCLECI
  CIRCLECI -->|"Continuous Integration"|> VERCEL_DEPLOY
  VERCEL_DEPLOY -->|"Deployment"|> VERCEL
  SHELL -->|"Scripting"|> CIRCLECI
  BATCHFILE -->|"Scripting"|> CIRCLECI
  class NEXT, REACT, MDX, CSS, SCSS, SASS, PUG client
  class VERCEL, NGINX, AUTH gateway
  class PYTHON, NODE, RUST, WEBASSEMBLY service
  class DATABASE, MONGODB, GRAPHQL data
  class GITHUB, CIRCLECI, VERCEL_DEPLOY, SHELL, BATCHFILE infra
data.readme:
**The React Framework for Production-Ready Applications** [![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-yellow.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT) [![Stars: 138229](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/vercel/next.js?style=social)](https://github.com/vercel/next.js) [![Language: JavaScript](https://img.shields.io/badge/Language-JavaScript-blue)](https://github.com/vercel/next.js) ## 📑 Table of Contents 1. [Introduction](#what-is-nextjs) 2. [Features](#features) 3. [Architecture](#architecture) 4. [Quick Start / Setup](#quick-start--setup) 5. [Configuration](#configuration) 6. [Usage Examples](#usage-examples) 7. [Contributing](#contributing) 8. [License](#license) ## 🎯 What is Next.js? Next.js is a popular React framework for building server-rendered, statically generated, and performance-optimized web applications. It provides a robust set of features for building fast, scalable, and maintainable applications. With Next.js, developers can create complex web applications with ease, leveraging the power of React and a set of built-in features for routing, internationalization, and optimization. ## ✨ Features * **Server-Side Rendering (SSR):** Next.js allows for server-side rendering of React components, providing fast page loads and improved SEO. * **Static Site Generation (SSG):** Next.js can generate static HTML files for your application, reducing the need for server-side rendering and improving performance. * **Internationalization (i18n):** Next.js provides built-in support for internationalization, making it easy to create multilingual applications. * **Routing:** Next.js includes a built-in routing system, allowing for easy creation of client-side and server-side routes. * **Optimization:** Next.js provides a range of optimization features, including code splitting, minification, and compression. ## 🏗️ Architecture The Next.js architecture consists of the following layers: * **Frontend:** React/Next.js - responsible for rendering the user interface and handling client-side logic. * **Backend:** Python - responsible for handling server-side logic and API requests. * **Database:** Not detected - Next.js does not include a built-in database, allowing developers to choose their own database solution. * **Caching:** Not detected - Next.js does not include built-in caching, but developers can use external caching solutions. * **DevOps:** Not detected - Next.js does not include built-in DevOps tools, but developers can use external tools for deployment, monitoring, and logging. ## 🚀 Quick Start / Setup To get started with Next.js, follow these steps: ```bash # Install Node.js and npm curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_lts.x | sudo -E bash - sudo apt-get install -y nodejs # Install Next.js npm install next # Create a new Next.js project npx create-next-app my-app # Start the development server cd my-app npm run dev ``` ## ⚙️ Configuration Next.js provides a range of configuration options, including: | Option | Description | | --- | --- | | `target` | Specify the target browser or Node.js version | | `webpack` | Customize the Webpack configuration | | `babel` | Customize the Babel configuration | | `env` | Set environment variables | ## 📖 Usage Examples Here are some examples of using Next.js: ```bash # Create a new page touch pages/about.js # Create a new API route touch pages/api/users.js # Use the `getStaticProps` method for static site generation import { GetStaticProps } from 'next'; function HomePage({ data }) { return
{data}
; } export const getStaticProps: GetStaticProps = async () => { const data = await fetch('https://api.example.com/data'); return { props: { data: await data.json(), }, }; }; ``` ## 🤝 Contributing Next.js is an open-source project, and contributions are welcome. To contribute, follow these steps: 1. Fork the Next.js repository 2. Create a new branch for your feature or bug fix 3. Submit a pull request with a detailed description of your changes ## 📄 License Next.js is licensed under the MIT license. See the LICENSE file for details.
